{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 0.6, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Independence, Constitution, and first universal-franchise elections", "reasoning": "Universal suffrage, the 1951-52 elections, an independent press/judiciary, and mass mobilization (e.g., Andhra agitation) strengthened society's structural leverage, while the new republic consolidated a unified bureaucracy, army, and integrated the princely states\u2014yet the shock of Partition, loss of the colonial coercive apparatus, low fiscal reach, and mass poverty left the state strong but somewhat less penetrating than the wartime colonial peak."}
